private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Orbit Fab, a Series A-stage space and aerospace company, has raised $28.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 private-sector peers at the same or adjacent stage—ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), and Hubble Network ($400M Series B)—alongside 5 public Space & Aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $28.5M raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $57M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $850M, with a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures for the target. The public sector median P/S multiple stands at 64.2x across 5 traded peers, but this requires private revenue data to operationalize.

The triangulated valuation range is $48M to $977M, with a median of $454M. The wide spread reflects stage and maturity heterogeneity within the peer set: ClearSpace and Hubble Network anchor the lower bound at $400M (Series A), while Cowboy Space and Aalyria at $2.0B and $1.3B (Series B) anchor the upper range. Key uncertainties include Orbit Fab's revenue trajectory, unit economics in on-orbit refueling, and the timing and scale of Series B deployment capital.
